channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UK gives sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius. The US base on Diego Garcia stays
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government agreed Thursday to hand sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands, an archipelago of more than 60 islands in the Indian Ocean, to Mauritius, in a deal to secure the future of a strategically important U.K.-U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Peru celebrates 2 decades of a fast-growing breed of guinea pigs eaten as a delicacy
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru on Thursday celebrated two decades since the creation of a genetically modified breed of guinea pig, a rodent whose meat has formed a part of the diet of people in the Andean nation for thousands of years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: UN peacekeepers stay on Lebanon's border despite Israeli ground incursion
item.description: The United Nations peacekeeping chief says U.N. peacekeepers are staying in their positions on Lebanon’s southern border despite Israel’s request to vacate some areas before it launched its ground operation.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: North Korea says leader Kim Jong Un has threatened to use nuclear weapons and destroy South Korea permanently if provoked.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a "listening device" in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017. Johnson had been a foreign secretary at the time.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil’s proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US organizing flights out of Lebanon, offering loans for tickets
item.description: The U.S. is organizing flights out of Lebanon to help evacuate its citizens and offering loans for those who cannot purchase flight tickets. So far, around 250 Americans and their family members have departed Lebanon this week on two flights organized by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: At least 78 people died when a boat with 278 on board capsized in eastern Congo, official says
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 78 people died when an overcrowded boat capsized on Lake Kivu in eastern Congo on Thursday, a local governor said. A frantic search and rescue operation was underway hours later as many remained unaccounted for from the vessel, believed to have had 278 people on board.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Africa's top court will decide if impeachment proceedings against the president can be revived
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South Africa's Constitutional Court said Thursday that it would hear a case next month brought by two opposition parties to revive impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over a scandal involving more than $500,000 in cash that was hidden in a couch a
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Harpole Treasure includes one of the most valuable pieces of ancient jewelry found in Britain
item.description: The Harpole Treasure was discovered in 2022, and refers to a collection of valuable relics unearthed from a burial ground in England. Among the finds was a unique piece of jewelry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus opposition urges immediate release of over 200 political prisoners in dire state
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Belarusian opposition leader in exile Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya on Thursday said 224 political prisoners in Belarus must be freed urgently, and called on Western countries to negotiate their release with the Belarusian authorities.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US bans steel, artificial sweetener imports from Chinese companies over forced labor allegations
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security announced Wednesday it will no longer import steel or artificial sweeteners from two companies based in China due to their alleged use of forced labor. The department said Baowu Group Xinjiang Bayi Iron and Steel Co. Ltd.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Typhoon Krathon makes landfall in Taiwan, packing fierce winds and torrential rain
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Typhoon Krathon made landfall Thursday in Taiwan's major port city of Kaohsiung, bringing torrential rains and fierce winds to the island's south. Trees were brought down by high winds and roads were flooded, prompting the closure of schools and businesses.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military says it killed a senior Hamas leader in a Gaza strike earlier this year
item.description: The Israeli military has warned people to evacuate a city and other communities in southern Lebanon that are north of a U.N.-declared buffer zone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan hospital fire leaves at least 9 dead as typhoon batters island's south
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A fire at a hospital in southern Taiwan killed at least nine people Thursday as the island was being battered by a typhoon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two boats carrying migrants sink in the Red Sea off Djibouti's coast killing 45, UN says
item.description: DJIBOUTI (AP) — Two vessels carrying migrants from Africa sank in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti, killing 45 people, the U.N. migration agency said Tuesday. The boats had departed from Yemen carrying 310 people, the International Organization for Migration said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum keeps doing morning briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President Claudia Sheinbaum started her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning news briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda records at least 9 deaths from Marburg outbreak
item.description: Rwanda has declared a public outbreak of the Marburg virus, confirming nearly 30 infections and at least nine deaths as of the end of September.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Catholic reform debate launched by Pope Francis leans away from ordained roles for women
item.description: ROME (AP) — Debate was leaning away from allowing women to take on ordained roles in the Catholic Church as the second phase of Pope Francis’ reform project opened Wednesday with an agenda topped by calls for women to take up more positions of responsibility Francis presided over an opening Mass in
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 Russian journalists accused of working for a Navalny group go on trial in Moscow
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Four Russian journalists went on trial in Moscow on Wednesday after being accused of working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late Russian opposition politician Alexei Navalny, which was designated by authorities as an extremist organization in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will Netanyahu 'take the fight directly to Iran' after massive missile strike across Israel?
item.description: Amid the most lethal Iranian missile strike into Israel territory, military experts are urging Israel to take off the gloves and punish the mullahs in Iran with devastating warfare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 dead and thousands evacuated as a typhoon approaches Taiwan
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — An approaching typhoon bringing strong winds and torrential rainfall to Taiwan killed two people and injured dozens of others over the past few days and forced thousands to evacuate from low-lying or mountainous areas.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU and JOHNSON LAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli troops battle Hezbollah in Lebanon as fears of a wider war mount after Iran's missile attack
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli troops battled Hezbollah militants inside Lebanon on Wednesday after launching what they say are limited ground operations there.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli military says regular infantry, armored units joining limited ground operation in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said regular infantry and armored units were joining what it has called its limited and localized ground operations in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran says it fired dozens of missiles into Israel, where residents are warned to shelter in place
item.description: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel. Israel warned Lebanese people earlier Tuesday to evacuate border communities to avoid harm from limited ground operations it said were launched against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah's acting leader vows to fight on after Nasrallah's death
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah's acting leader vowed Monday to continue battling Israel and said the militant group was prepared for a long fight even after much of its top command was wiped out, including its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iranian operatives charged with hacking Trump's presidential campaign
item.description: The operatives are accused of hacking Trump's campaign and disseminating stolen information to media organizations.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
